title: Getting 'sharredString's when trying to parse XSLX in the streaming mode
author: johnmalkovich100
body:
Sometimes, when I'm trying to use https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs#reading-xlsx for reading rows in the streaming mode, instead of list of values, I'm getting a list of 'sharredString's

```
[
   { sharedString: 1 },
   { sharedString: 2 },
   { sharedString: 3 },
   ....
]
```

I noticed that there is a place when the library is trying to parse sharedString section https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs/blob/master/lib/stream/xlsx/workbook-reader.js#L84
but looks like it doesn't map them back to row values.

Is it defined behaviour?

comments.author: LesterLyu
comments.body: Can you try ```Excel.xlsx.readFile()```. From my understanding, stream mode has many limitations and bugs, it is a totally different code from ```Excel.xlsx```.

comments.author: johnmalkovich100
comments.body: @LesterLyu `Excel.xlsx.readFile()` is working, but unfortunately I need the stream mode because of my particular case where the file is big and need to read only first few lines.

comments.author: danieler1981
comments.body:
I have the same issue while trying to read a XLSX in streaming mode.
It's also hard to find a good example of StreamingReader - so maybe I'm using the reader in a wrong way.

comments.author: aksharj
comments.body:
Hi,

Is there any update on this, i am also getting `SharedString` when accessing `row.values` in stream mode on the row event.

I am trying to read the excel file in stream mode and create / write to another excel file with few modifications. Thanks.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
probably related to 
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1431

comments.author: BigKamil5
comments.body: I think it might be related to https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1558#issuecomment-1819842654

comments.author: Nick-Lucas
comments.body:
> probably related to #1431

The solution proposed there doesn't solve the issue for me. I guess workbook reader isn't reliable at all

comments.author: Sloff
comments.body: I'm also getting this issue intermittently when using the Stream reader

comments.author: moonjoungyoung
comments.body:
It is intermittently happening.
Same setting, Same file, Different result.

comments.author: baian1
comments.body: same issuse, run test `pnpm mocha --require spec/config/setup spec\integration\issues\issue-1328-xlsx-worksheet-reader-date.spec.js`, it can reproduce

comments.author: moonjoungyoung
comments.body:
Any updates?
I think it is a critical issue.

comments.author: AnechaS
comments.body: I created [PR 2915](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/pull/2915) to fix this issue.

comments.author: SHG-James
comments.body: Is there any updates for this? I have the same problem, but the issue is intermittent, sometimes it read correctly, but sometimes it return `sharedStrings`

comments.author: thomack
comments.body: Having the same issue, I randomly get a sharedStrings object. Does anyone have a solution about that ?

comments.author: morasyad
comments.body: any update?

comments.author: gerpedrosa
comments.body:
This bug is still present in version 4.4.0.  
I tested the fix proposed in PR #2915  and can confirm it solves the issue.  
Would it be possible to review and consider merging it in a future release?

comments.author: RacceGatel
comments.body: I have the same problem. The project hasn't been updated for a long time. Are there any forks with new fixes?

comments.author: Silentscripter
comments.body:
For anyone still finding this issue we have identified the dependency that causes this side effect which is also the reason for this bug to be not reproducible in every context.
**tmp** version  >= **0.2.4**  make this happen while we managed to work with read streams correctly with **tmp@0.2.3** version.
Still didn't have time to understand what change exactly is causing this bug into exceljs.

comments.author: Jv7680
comments.body:
Hi!

When will the fix PR be merged? I am encountering the same issue.

For anyone who needs a workaround, the only solution that works for me is downgrading the ExcelJS version from 4.4.0 to 4.2.0 Hope this helps.

comments.author: aawnu
comments.body: Any updates on this? It's seems to be a very inconsistent error, I have files with hundreds of lines with no issues and files with 4 lines where its `{sharedString: X}`.

comments.author: protobi-pieter
comments.body: @aawnu @Jv7680 You can use https://www.npmjs.com/package/@protobi/exceljs  which resolves this issue (merging and reviewing the above PR among many others) and is a drop-in replacement for ExcelJS. You might also look at ExcelTS.
